Biswa1,2 , Hiroshi Mori2,3 , Atsushi Toyoda4 , Ken Kurokawa2,5 , Tsuyoshi Koide1,2 1 Mouse...Genomics...Resource...Laboratory,...National...Institute...of...Genetics,...Mishima,...Shizuoka,...Japan,... 2 Department...of...Genetics,...Graduate...University...for...Advanced...Studies,...SOKENDAI,...Mishima,...Shizuoka,...Japan,... 3 Genome...Diversity...Laboratory,...National...Institute...of...Genetics,...Mishima,...Shizuoka,...Japan,... 4 Comparative...Genomics...Laboratory,...National...Institute...of...Genetics,...Mishima,...Shizuoka,...Japan,... 5 Genome...Evolution...Laboratory,...National...Institute...of...Genetics,...Mishima,...Shizuoka,...Japan Blockage of apolipoprotein E and amyloid -protein precursor interaction reduces Alzheimer pathology Masakazu Fujiwara, Naoki Koyama, Takashi Mori Department...of...Biomedical...Sciences,...Saitama...Medical...Center,...Saitama,...Japan P-113 P-114 --S 134-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 134 69...... [Introduction] As... the... current... super-aging... society... approaches,... Alzheimer's... disease... (AD)... has... become... a... worldwide... public... health... concern.... Previously,... it... has... reported...that...a...cholesterol...transporter,...apolipoprotein...E... (apoE),...interacts...with...amyloid... -protein...precursor...(APP),... which... enhances... amyloid-(A)... production... as... an... alternative...amyloidogenic...pathway....In...this...regard,...we... identified...the...specific...site...of...this...interaction...corresponding... to... low... density... lipoprotein... receptor... (LDLR)... binding... domain...of...apoE...(aa...133-152,...ApoEp),...and...further...designed... a... peptide... to... block... the... apoE-APP... interaction... by... the... addition...of...six...lysine...residues...to...the...N-terminal...ApoEp... (6KApoEp). [Objects] Having...identified...a...peptide...that...interacts...with... APP...instead...of...apoE...and...discovered...a...designer...peptide... that...blocks...this...interaction,...we...investigated...the...effects...of... ApoEp...and...6KApoEp...peptides...on...AD-like...pathology...and... behavioral... impairment... in... 3XTg-AD... and... 5XFAD... transgenic...mice,...respectively. [Materials and Methods] At... 6... weeks... of... age,... we... intraperitoneally...administered...ApoEp...(250...g/kg)...or... vehicle...once...daily...for...3...months...to...3XTg-AD...transgenic... mice....Moreover,...at...9...months...of...age,...6KApoEp...(250...g/ kg)...or...vehicle...was...intraperitoneally...administered...once... daily...for...3...months...to...5XFAD...transgenic...mice....After... 3-month... therapy,... AD-relevant... phenotypes... were... investigated...in...these...Alzheimer...mice. [Results]... Promoting... apoE... interaction... with... APP... by... ApoEp...exacerbated...A...and...tau...brain...pathologies...in... 3XTg-AD... transgenic... mice,... whereas... disrupting... this... interaction...by...6KApoEp...ameliorated...cerebral...A...and... tau...pathologies,...neuronal...apoptosis,...synaptic...loss,...and... behavioral...impairment...in...5XFAD...transgenic...mice. [Conclusion] These...data...highlight...that...disrupting...apoE... interaction...with...APP...may...be...a...new...strategy...for...AD... therapy. Gangliosides... are... glycosphingolipids... that... have... been... modified...by...various...glycosyltransferases...from...ceramide.... Gangliosides...are...the...major...glycolipids...in...the...brain...and... are...suggested...to...have...the...following...functions...in...neuronal... maturation.:...1)...neurite...formation,...2)...axon...outgrowth,...and...3)... myelin... formation.... Previously,... we... showed... that... two... glycosyltransferases,...4-galactosyltransferases...5...and...6,... are...essential...for...the...synthesis...of...lactosylceramide,...the... starting...point...of...gangliosides...synthesis,...in...the...mouse... brain...by...analyzing...mice...lacking...the...two...enzymes...in...the... whole...brain...(Yoshihara...et...al.,...2018)....Since...these...deficient... mice... die... around... the... weaning... period,... we... generated... telencephalon...specific...knockout...(KO)...mice...using...Emx1... Cre...mice...to...analyze...higher...brain...functions....These...KO... mice...have...reached...adulthood,...and...can...be...subjected...to... behavioral...test...battery,...which...measures...activity,...emotion,... learning/memory,...attention,...and...motor...functions....In...the... previous...meeting,...we...reported...that...the...KO...mice...had... behavioral...characteristics...such...as...hyperactivity,...enhanced... attention. Here...we...will...report...the...investigation...of...the...material... bases... in... the... brain... underlying... the... behavioral... characteristics...of...these...KO...mice....It...is...known...that...the... activity...depends...on...the...motor...control...circuits...so-called... direct...and...indirect...pass...ways...which...include...the...cerebral... cortex,... striatum,... and... substantia... nigra,... and... that... the... attention... are... also... controlled... by... the... dopamine... (DA)... system....We...conducted...an...open-field...test...in...which...KO... mice...were...treated...with...DA...receptor...drugs...that...can... reduce... synapse... DA... and... found... that... both... drugs... significantly...attenuated...hyperactivity....In...the...prepulse... inhibition...test,...enhanced...attention...was...also...reduced...by... these...drugs,...suggesting...that...the...DA...system...function...was... enhanced...in...these...KO...mice....In...addition,...the...primary... cortical...culture...and...histological...analysis...showed...that...the... cortical...neurons...were...immature....These...results...suggest... that...the...neural...circuitry...changes,...including...DA...system,... mainly...caused...by...the...immaturity...of...the...cortex...are...the... cause...of...the...behavioral...changes...in...KO...mice.... A new therapeutic agent for Alzheimer's disease: an apolipoprotein E-derived peptide (6KApoEp) Naoki Koyama, Masakazu Fujiwara, Takashi Mori Department...of...Biomedical...Sciences,...Saitama...Medical...Center Analysis of activity and attentional function in mice lacking telencephalon specific gangliosides Toru Yoshihara,1 Koichi Furukawa2 , Masahide Asano1 1 Institute...for...Laboratory...Animals,...Graduate...School...of...Medicine,...Kyoto...University,... 2 College...of...Life...and...Health...Sciences,...Chubu...University P-115 P-116 --S 135-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 135 69...... The...limbic...system...is...located...underneath...the...cerebral... cortex... and... above... the... brainstem... and... includes... the... thalamus....ST3...beta-galactoside...alpha-2,3-sialyltransferase... 4... (St3gal4)... is... a... stimulus-responsive...enzyme... and... the... deficiency...induces...anxiety-...and...depression-like...behaviors... in... mice.... As... St3gal4... mRNA... expresses... in... the... medial... geniculate...highly...in...the...thalamus,...we...hypothesized...that... the...deficiency...might...also...affect...the...startle...response...and... prepulse...inhibition...(PPI)....The...PPI...is...a...phenomenon...in... which...a...weak...prepulse...attenuates...the...response...to...a... subsequent...startling...stimulus...and...the...PPI...occurs...when... the...prepulse...blocks...the...gate...at...the...entrance...of...the...brain... sensory... input.... Decreased... PPI... has... been... observed... in... humans...suffering...from...schizophrenia...and...ADHD,...and... animal...models...of...schizophrenia....It...has...been...reported...that... some...antipsychotic...drugs...are...adequate...for...the...decrease... in...PPI,...such...as...haloperidol,... a...dopamine... D2...receptor... antagonist,...and...clozapine,...a...dopamine...D2...receptor...and... serotonin...2A/2C...receptor...antagonist. Here,... we... administered... haloperidol... (1.0 mg/kg)... and... clozapine...(1.5mg/kg)...sequentially...and...intraperitoneally... with...two...weeks...intervals,...then...mice...received...a...set...of... startle...and...PPI...test....There...were...no...differences...between... KO...and...wild-type...mice...in...the...startle...test...regardless...of... drugs,...respectively....On...the...other...hand,...both...haloperidol... and...clozapine...recovered...the...PPI...level...of...KO...mice...to...that... of...wild-type...mice,...respectively....However,...the...numbers... and... intensities... of... dopaminergic... neurons... were... not... changed...between...KO...and...wild-type...mice,...while...those...of... serotonergic...neurons...tended...to...increase...in...KO...mice.... Next,...we...investigated...neurons...that...auditory...PPI...activates... by...the...immunofluorescence...with...anti...c-fos...(neuronal... activated...marker)...antibody...in...C57BL/6J...male...mice....We... found... that... the... cerebral... cortex... and... substantia... nigra... reticular...neurons...increased...the...number...of...c-fos-positive... cells.... We... currently... investigate... the... number... of... c-fos- positive...neurons...in...the...cerebral...cortices...in...KO...and...wild- type...mice...and...expression...of...dopamine...D2...receptors...or... serotonin...2A/2C...receptors...in...neurons...of...KO...mice. Protein-protein... interaction... (PPI)... is... essential... for... the... proper... regulation... of... cellular... and... tissue... functions.... Proximity-dependent...biotin...labeling...(BioID)...is...a...powerful... method...to...analyze...PPI...and...has...been...widely...used...in vitro.... BirA...(R118G,...hereafter...designated...BirA*),...a...biotin...ligase,... is...fused...to...a...protein...of...interest...and...is...expressed...in...living... cells....The...BirA*-fusion...protein...can...biotinylate...proximal... proteins... depending... on... the... treatment... with... high... concentration...of...biotin,...enabling...a...comprehensive...analysis... of...PPI....However,...the...usage...of...BioID...in...mice...has...been... limited....Thus,...there...is...little...information...regarding...an... appropriate... method... for... biotin... administration... or... availability...of...other...tissues....In...this...study,...we...generated... novel...biotin...ligase...knock-in...mouse...strains...to...establish...in... vivo...BioID...assay....Furthermore,...we...tried...to...identify...the... cerebral...proteins...associated...with...depression-like...behavior. (1)...We...generated...ROSA26-BMAL1-BioID...knock-in...mice,... which... systemically... express... a... BMAL1-BirA*... fusion... protein....To...induce...protein...biotinylation...in...living...mice,...we... fed...BMAL1-BioID...mice...a...diet...containing...0.5%...biotin...for...7... days...and...found...that...biotin...feeding...markedly...increased... protein...biotinylation...in...the...brain,...heart,...testis...and...liver...of... BMAL1-BioID...mice. (2)...Usp46...has...been...identified...as...a...regulatory...gene...of... mouse... depression-like... behavior.... To... explore... the... PPI... network... of... USP46,... we... generated... Usp46-miniTurbo... knock-in...mice....MiniTurbo...is...a...highly...active...biotin...ligase... variant....The...biotin-feeding...for...7...days...markedly...increased... protein...biotinylation...in...the...brain...of...Usp46-miniTurbo... knock-in... mice.... Mass... spectrometry... analysis... of... the... biotinylated...proteins...identified...95...candidate...interactants... of...USP46. In...conclusion,...we...established...a...new...method...for...in...vivo... biotin... labeling... by... biotin-feeding.... In... addition,... we... successfully...identified...a...novel...protein...network...for...the... regulation...of...depression-like...behavior...in...the...brain.... Effect of St3gal4 deficiency in the thalamic sensory relay neurons on auditory prepulse inhibition Tatsuya Kusunoki, Toshiya Tamura, Haruna Hirota, Chisa Ota, Keiko Kato Division...of...life...sciences...,...Kyoto...Sangyo...Univ Uncovering a novel protein network related to depression-like behavior by in vivo BioID Kazuya Murata1 , Yoko Daitoku2 , Seiya Mizuno1 , Fumihiro Sugiyama1 1 Faculty...of...medicine,...University...of...Tsukuba,...2 Laboratory...animal...resource...center,...University...of...Tsukuba P-117 P-118 --S 136-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 136 69...... Neurobehavior profile in canines with Duchenne muscular dystrophy Eri Takeuchi1 , Yasumasa Hashimoto1 , Michihiro Imamura1 , Takefumi Kikusui2 , Masayuki Sekiguchi1,2 , Yoshitsugu Aoki1 1 Department...of...Molecular...Therapy,...National...Institute...of...Neuroscience,...National...Center...of...Neurology...and... Psychiatry,...2 School...of...Veterinary...Medicine,...Azabu...University,...3 Faculty...of...Pharmaceutical...Science,...Tokyo... University...of...Science P-119 Duchenne...muscular...dystrophy...(DMD)...is...an...X-linked... muscular... disease... that... presents... progressive... muscle... wasting... caused... by... a... mutation... in... the... DMD... gene.... Additionally,...30%...of...DMD...shows...psychiatric...disorders,... such... as... autism... and... high... anxiety,... due... to... a... lack... of... dystrophin...from...the...brain....We...previously...reported...that... mdx,...a...mouse...model...of...DMD,...lacks...the...full-length...Dp427... in...its...muscles...and...brain...and...exhibits...the...fear...response... associated...with...a...lack...of...Dp427...from...the...brain....The... canine...X-linked...muscular...dystrophy...in...Japan...(CXMDJ)...is... a...middle-sized...model...of...DMD...and...lacks...Dp427...in...muscles... and...brain...due...to...a...point...mutation...in...the...DMD...intron...6... splice...acceptor...site....They...also...show...neurobehavioral... comorbidities....However,...how...brain...dystrophin...absence... impacts... neurobehavioral... comorbidities... are... still... not... understood...in...CXMDJ....Here,...we...examined...the...expression... levels...and...localization...of...Dp427...in...the...brain...of...CXMDJ... and...wild-type...beagle...dogs...using...western...blotting...and... immunohistochemistry....We...confirmed...that...Dp427...was... abolished...from...the...cortex,...hippocampus,...amygdala,...and... cerebellum...in...CXMDJ....We...also...performed...a...series...of... behavior...tests...and...found...that...CXMDJ...showed...anxiety- like...behavior...similar...to...the...mdx...mice,...probably...due...to...a... lack...of...Dp427....Our...data...strongly...suggests...that...a...lack...of... Dp427...in...CXMDJ...causes...anxiety-like...behavior. Obesity,...diabetes...mellitus,...cardiovascular...disease,...and... metabolic...disorders...are...markedly...increased...with...the... onset...of...psychiatric...disorders....However,...the...pathogenic... mechanism...of...comorbidity...has...been...poorly...understood... due...to...the...lack...of...model...mice. In...this...study,...we...used...an...amygdala...kindled...mouse...model,... which...shows...symptoms...similar...with...human...temporal... lobe...epilepsy....When...stimulation...was...continued...after...the... onset...of...the...seizures,...body...weight...began...to...increase... within...1...week,...glucose...intolerance...was...observed...within...3... weeks.... Insulin... resistant... index,... HOMA-IR... level... was... increased...within...7...weeks....The...weight...of...white...adipose... tissue,...brown...adipose...tissue,...and...brain...were...increased,... while...there...was...no...change...in...food...intake,...suggesting...that... the...changes...in...glucose...and...lipid...metabolism...induced... obesity. However,...plasma...biochemical...assay...revealed...that...there... was... no... change... in... total... triglyceride... levels,... total... cholesterol,...or...free...fatty...acids,...while...free...glycerol...levels... were...elevated....Next,...we...hypothesized...that...the...increase... of...blood...glucose...level...by...amygdala...stimulation...produced... obesity.... Using... amygdala-kindled...mice... that... acquired... epileptic...seizures,...we...measured...the...blood...glucose...levels... before...and...after...amygdala...stimulation....As...a...result,...we... found...a...significant...increase...in...blood...glucose...levels...up...to... 90...minutes...after...stimulation...and...an...increase...in...plasma... insulin...at...60...minutes....It...suggested...that...the...increase...in... adipose...tissue...was...due...to...a...combination...of...factors:...the... increase...in...blood...glucose...levels...promoted...the...glycolysis,... which...results...in...glyceroneogenesis,...and...lipogenesis;...and... lipolysis...was...suppressed...by...insulin. These...findings...indicate...that...this...epileptic...mouse...model...is... a...model...of...comorbidity,...in...which...hyperglycemia...leads...to... obesity...upon...amygdala...stimulation. The association of obesity and diabetes in amygdala stimulated temporal lobe epilepsy mouse model Tomoya Tanaka, Akiko Fujita, Yasuhiro Togawa, Manami Ota, Keiko Kato Division...of...life...sciences,...Kyoto...Sangyo...Univ P-120 --S 137-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 137 69...... Recent...bioinformatics...and...high-throughput...sequencing... studies... reported... that... many... long... noncoding... RNAs... (lncRNAs)...possess...short..."non-canonical"...open...reading... frame...(sORFs)... encoding... microproteins.... These... newly... identified...microproteins...are...expected...to...be...novel...factors,... which...can...be...missing...pieces...of...our...understanding...of...life... science...and...also...potential...targets...of...drug...discovery....The... Tcl1...upstream...neuron-associated...RNA...(TUNAR)...was... discovered... as... a... lncRNA.... By... in... silico... screening,... we... however...identified...that...TUNAR... has... a...sORF... region... encoding...a...48-amino...acid...polypeptide...with...high...sequence... conservation....The...lncRNA...TUNAR...plays...a...vital...role...in... pluripotency... and... neural... differentiation... of... mouse... embryonic... stem... cells.... Whether... or... not... the... sORF... is... translated...into...a...microprotein...in...vivo,...and...how...TUNAR... and...the...microprotein...affect...brain...function...and...behaviors,... however,...remain...unclear. Here,...we...generated...TUNAR-microprotein...reporter...mice... and...TUNAR...sORF...deletion...mice....Utilizing...TUNAR- microprotein... reporter... mice... in... which... an... epitope-tag... coding...sequence...was...inserted...before...the...stop...codon,...we... detected...the...epitope...tag-specific...stained...cells...in...the... mouse...central...nervous...system....These...findings...suggested... that...the...TUNAR...sORF...is...endogenously...translated...into...a... protein...in...mice...brain....To...investigate...the...function...of... TUNAR...microprotein...in...the...brain,...we...subjected...TUNAR... sORF...deletion...mice...to...a...comprehensive...behavioral...test... battery....In...the...startle...response/pre-pulse...inhibition...(PPI)... test,...while...there...was...no...significant...difference...in...the... acoustic...startle...response,...TUNAR...sORF...deletion...mice... showed...increased...PPI...compared...with...WT...mice....In...both... the...Porsolt...forced...swim...test...and...tail...suspension...test,... TUNAR... sORF... deletion... mice... exhibited... increased... depression-related...behavior....These...findings...together... indicate... that... the... non-canonical... microprotein... from... TUNAR...is...translated...in...the...brain...and...has...a...critical...role... in...sensory-motor...gating...and...depression-related...behavior. Depression... is... a... pervasive... disorder... that... displays... a... combination...of...symptoms,...including...reduced...motivation,... feeling...of...despair...and...appetite...loss....Lateral...habenula...(LHb),... which...is...regulator...of...monoamines...metabolism,...has...been... considered...as...a...crucial...brain...region...for...depression. Recently,...many...studies...have...indicated...that...inflammatory... responses...in...brain...are...implicated...in...the...pathophysiology... of... depression.... However,... little... is... known... about... the... underlying...molecular...mechanisims...of...crosstalk...between... neuroinflammation... and... depressive-like... behavioral... phenotypes....In...this...study,...we...evaluated...inflammatory... responses...in...LHb...and...performed...RNA...sequencing...on... LHb...with...chronic...social...defeat...stress...(CSDS)...as...a...model... for...depression.... We...found...that...CSDS...induces...monocyte...infiltration...and... pro-inflammatory...cytokine...production...in...LHb...of...defeated... mice...with...susceptible...phenotype....We...also...identified...up- regulation...of...Pro-protein...convertase...5...(Pcsk5)...in...LHb...of... susceptible... mouse... brains.... PCSK5... activated... Matrix... metalloproteinases...(MMPs)...and...enhanced...motility...in... monocyte.../...microglia....Further,...suppression...of...Pcsk5... improved... abnormal... depressive-like... behavior... and... pathological...phenotypes...in...the...CSDS...mouse...model....These... results... have... collectively... suggested... that... PCSK5... is... a... critical... regulator... of... inflammation... in... LHb... and... susceptibility...for...depression. Loss of non-canonical open reading frame within lncRNA TUNAR increased pre-pulse inhibition and depression-related behavior in mice Kazuki Fujii1,2 , Yusuke Moriwaki3 , Yumie Koshidaka2 , Mayumi Adachi2 , Yuko Yanagibashi2 , Shoko Hongo2 , Yasunori Aizawa4 , Keizo Takao1,2,3 1 Dept....Behav....Physiol.,...Fac....Med.,...Univ....Toyama,...Toyama,...2 ...Life...Sci...Res...Ctr,...Univ....Toyama,...Toyama,... 3 Dept....Behav....Physiol.,...Grad....Sch....Med....Pharma....Sci.,...Univ....Toyama,...Toyama, 4 Grad....Sch....Biosci....Biotech.,...Tokyo...Institute...of...Technology,...Yokohama Up-regulation of Pcsk5 in the mouse habenula causes depressive- like behaviors and neuroinflammation Hikaru Ito1,2,3 , Kanako Nozaki2,4 , Hidenori Aizawa2 1 Department...of...Life...Science...Research,...Faculty...of...Medicine,...Kagawa...University,...2 Department...of...Neurobiology,... Hiroshima...University,...3 Center...for...Experimental...Animals,...Tokyo...Medical...and...Dental...University,... 4 Division...of...Neuroanatomy,...Yamaguchi...University P-121 P-122 --S 138-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 138 69...... Social...facilitation...is...a...psychological...phenomenon...that...an... individual's... performance... improves... in... frequency... and... intensity...due...to...mere...presence...of...other...individuals,...and... affects...behaviors....We...previously...reported...that...voluntary... exercise...of...more...social...C57BL/6J,...but...not...less...social... BALB/cCrSlc...male...mice,...was...enhanced...in...the...presence... of... conspecific... observer... mice,... implicating... functional... deficiency... in... either... or... both... of... self-presentation... and... perception...of...others...in...BALB/cCrSlc...male...mice....To... clarify...which...is...the...functional...deficiency,...we...examined... voluntary... exercise... in... the... presence... of... heterospecific... observer...male...mice,...and...found...that...voluntary...exercise...of... more...social...C57BL/6J...male...mice...was...enhanced...in...the... presence...of...both...conspecific...C57BL/6J...and...heterospecific... BALB/cCrSlc...observer...male...mice....Diurnal...variation... analysis...revealed...that...enhanced...voluntary...exercise...by... conspecific...C57BL/6J...observer...male...mice...was...observed... during...the...dark...period,...while...that...by...heterospecific... BALB/cCrSlc...observer...male...mice...was...observed...during... the...light...and...dark...periods....These...results...suggest...that... each...conspecific...C57BL/6J...and...heterospecific...BALB/ cCrSlc...observer...male...mice...affect...social...facilitation,...that... is,...self-presentation...of...BALB/cCrSlc...male...mice...is...not... disabled....In...the...future,...we...examine...perception...of...others... in...less...social...BALB/cCrSlc...male...mice. [Background] Dystrophin...is...a...cytoskeletal...protein,...and... its... deficiency... causes... muscle... atrophy,... resulting... in... respiratory... and/or... cardiac... failure.... Dystrophin... is... expressed...even...in...neurons,...and...patients,...who...don't...have... dystrophin... (Duchenne... muscular... dystrophy:... DMD),... sometimes... exhibit... developmental... disabilities.... In... the... present... study,... we... hypothesized... that... dystrophin... deficiency... affects... neuronal... signaling... in... the... nucleus... tractus...solitarius...(NTS)...of...the...medulla...oblongata,...which... is...a...part...of...central...regulatory...sites...in...cardiovascular... function....Therefore,...we...performed...ex...vivo...patch-clamp... experiments... in... dystrophin... completely... deficient... rats... (W-Dmdem1Kykn ,...DMDRs)...to...elucidate...the...role...of...dystrophin... on...neuronal...excitability...and...synaptic...transmission...in...NTS... neurons. [Methods]...Male...DMDRs...(DMD...group)...and...their...wild... type...littermates...(WT...group),...8-12...weeks...old,...were...used.... After... removal... of... the... brainstem... under... isoflurane... anesthesia,... coronal... slices... of... 250... m... thickness... were... obtained...from...the...medulla...oblongata....Neurons...located...in... the...NTS...of...the...slice...were...used...to...the...whole-cell...patch- clamp...recording....Action...potential...firings...in...response...to... depolarizing...current...injections...and...events...of...spontaneous... excitatory...postsynaptic...currents...(sEPSCs)...were...analyzed... in...current-clamp...and...voltage-clamp...mode,...respectively. [Results] There... was... no... difference... in... neuronal... excitabilities,...such...as...membrane...resistance...and...action... potential...firings,...between...WT...and...DMD...groups....Although... sEPSC...amplitude...did...not...differ...between...groups,...sEPSC... frequency...was...higher...in...the...DMD...group...than...WT...group. [Discussion] Dystrophin...deficiency...altered...synaptic... transmission...in...the...NTS,...while...no...significant...effect...was... found...on...neuronal...excitability....The...higher...frequency...of... sEPSCs... may... indicate... an... increase... in... the... number... of... excitatory...synapses...and/or...synaptic...vesicles...in...DMDRs.... Since...present...results...were...from...DMDRs...already...showing... disease...symptoms,...further...studies...will...be...needed...to... clarify...whether...changes...in...synaptic...transmission...were... associated...with...disease...progression...rather...than...the...lack... of...dystrophin. Increased voluntary exercise of C57BL/6J mice by both conspecific and heterospecific observer mice Kaichi Yoshizaki, Motoki Tanaka, Machiko Iida, Masato Asai Department...of...Disease...Model,...Institute...for...Developmental...Research,...Aichi...Developmental...Disability...Center Role of dystrophin on neuronal signaling in the nucleus tractus solitarius of transgenic rats Akitoshi Chikamoto, Shin-ichi Sekizawa, Ryota Tochinai, Masayoshi Kuwahara Agricultural...and...Life...Sciences,...The...University...of...Tokyo P-123 P-124 --S 139-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 139 69...... [OBJECTIVES]...Common...marmosets...(Callithrix jacchus)... have...well-developed...brain...functions...and...advanced...social... skills....For...analysis...of...pathogenesis...in...marmosets,...models... of...neurological...diseases...that...develop...with...aging...require... assessment... of... long-term... behavioral... and... cognitive... changes....We...are...currently...developing...a...behavior...analysis... system...in...which...touch-panel...task...devices...are...connected... to...a...family-group...cage,...enabling...animals...to...perform...tasks... freely....We...evaluated...this...system...by...monitoring...changes... in...the...number...of...individuals...entering...a...compartment...and... performing...tasks. [METHODS] A...marmoset...family...(father......10...y,...mother... ...5...y,...offspring......2...y)...was...used...for...this...study....The... touch...panel...device...compartment...was...connected...to...a... family...cage...via...a...tube....Individual...identification...was...based... on...microchip...detection...using...an...RFID...reader...installed...at... the... compartment... entrance.... Training... tasks... were... performed...for...22days,...followed...by...the...delayed...match-to- sample...tasks...for...52days....In...training...tasks,...animals...were... rewarded... for... touching... shapes... displayed... on... a... touch... screen,...and...a...delayed...match-to-sample...task,...rewarded...for... touching...a...correct...image. [RESULTS]...All...animals...could...perform...the...task...without... any... human... intervention,... and... the... frequency... of... task... execution...was...increased...in...proportion...to...the...number...of... times...animals...entered...the...compartment....In...the...offspring,... task...performance...increased...at...5.72.1...days...average...after... the...start...of...training,...and...at...52...days...in...the...delayed...match- to-sample...task. [DISCUSSION]...In...this...study,...each...animal...was...able...to... learn...and...perform...the...task...voluntarily,...indicating...the... utility...of...the...developed...system....The...inflection...point...of... tasks...executed...per...compartment...entry...appeared...to...occur... when...animals...learned...the...task,...and...the...rate...of...correct... responses...increased....Comparison...of...the...time...between... changes...in...task...and...inflection...point...indicated...that...a... quantitative... comparison... of...the... spontaneous... learning... process...would...be...possible. [Background] Itch... is... induced... by... various... diseases... including...allergy,...haptic...disorder,...and...renal...disorders.... Itch... is... also... evoked... by... stressors.... Therefore,... itch... assessment...is...import...for...improving...animal...welfare...as... well...as...the...investigation...of...disease...models....Scratching... measurement... is... the... only... way... to... assess... non-verbal... animal's...itch....In...the...most...studies,...animal's...scratching...is... counted... manually... depending... on... observers'... eyes.... However,...it...is...laborious,...time-consuming,...low-throughput... and...lacks...in...objectivity...and...reproducibility....Recently,...the... development... of... artificial... intelligence... technology... is... noteworthy....Especially,...convolutional...neural...network... (CNN),... mimicking... animal's... neural... circuits... exhibited... outstanding...performance...in...image...recognition...tasks....In... this... study,... we... aimed... to... automatically... detect... mouse... scratching... from... recorded... videos... using... convolution... recurrent...neural...network...(CRNN,...CNN...extended...along... time-axis). [Results] We... intradermally... injected... a... pruritogen,... lysophosphatidic...acid...(200...nmole)...to...BALB/c...mice...and... recorded...their...behavior....We...obtained...thirty...of...10-minutes... video...files....Videos...were...split...into...frame...images...and... cropped... around... the... geometric... center... of... mouse.... We... scrutinized...whether...each...frame...represented...scratching... or...not...by...manual...observation....We...constructed...CRNN...and... trained... it... using... 20... recorded... video... files.... We... then... evaluated...the...performance...of...trained...CRNN...using...resting... 10... files... which... was... not... used... for... training.... CRNN... successfully...predicted...the...scratching...of...mouse,...and...its... sensitivity...and...positive...predictive...rate...(PPR)...were...81.6%... and...87.9%...respectively....We...finally...evaluated...whether... trained...CRNN...could...be...applicable...to...another...pathological... model.... We... generated... dinitrofluorobenzene-induced... dermatitis... model... mice.... Trained... CRNN... predicted... scratching...of...these...mice...with...high...sensitivity...and...PPR... (94.8%...and...82,1%...respectively)....We...also...confirmed...that... the... number... and... duration... of... scratching... bouts... were... correlated...highly...between...human...observation...and...CRNN... prediction. [Conclusion] We...here...established...automated...mouse... scratching...detection...method...using...artificial...intelligence.... We...also...showed...this...method...can...be...applicable...to...the... assessment...of...pathological...mouse...models. Development of a cage-connected touch panel task system for family reared marmosets Rika Kikuchi1 , Terumi Yurimito1 , Fumiko Seki2 , Takashi Inoue1 , Kenya Sato1 , Wakako Kumita1 , Yumi Hayasegawa1 , Atsushi Iriki3 , Yumiko Yamazaki3 , Erika Sasaki1 1 Department...of...Marmoset...Biology...and...Medicine,...Central...Institute...for...Experimental...Animals,... 2 Live...Animal...Imaging...Center,...Central...Institute...for...Experimental...Animals,... 3 Center...for...Biosystems...Dynamics...Research,...Institute...of...Physical...and...Chemical...Research,...RIKEN The automated scratching detection method of mice using convolutional recurrent neural network Koji Kobayashi1 , Seiji Matsushita1 , Naoyuki Shimizu1 , Sakura Masuko1 , Masahito Yamamoto2 , Takahisa Murata1 1 Animal...Radiology,...The...University...of...Tokyo,...2 Autonomous...Systems...Engineering...Laboratory,...Hokkaido...University P-125 P-126 --S 140-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 140 69...... Feeding...behavior...is...regulated...by...a...neural...network...on...the... hypothalamus....The...hypothalamus...senses...feeding...control... hormones...(leptin...and...ghrelin,...etc.)...and...nutrients,...and... plays...a...role...in...maintaining...a...constant...energy...balance... throughout... the... body.... The... midbrain... controls... food... preferences...such...as...fat...and...sugar...through...dopaminergic... nerves....In...addition,...the...cerebral...cortex...has...been...found...to... link...to...food...seeking...behavior....On...the...other...hand,...anorexia... and...hyperorexia...are...thought...to...relate...to...psychological... stress...and...depression,...but...the...neural...and...molecular... mechanisms...of...such...abnormal...eating...behavior...remain... unclear. We... have... been... studying... endoplasmic... reticulum... (ER)... stress...in...mammals...and...have...had...many...opportunities...to... analyze...ER...stress...of...mice...in vivo....Tunicamycin...is...a...drug... frequently...used...in...mammalian...culture...cells...and...yeast... cells,... as... well... as... in... mice... for... ER... stress... treatment.... Surprisingly,...tunicamycin...also...induces...overeating...in...mice.... Therefore,...we...hypothesized...that...ER...stress...responsive... molecules...may...be...involved...in...the...regulation...of...feeding... behavior. We...conducted...overfeeding...induction...experiments...using... mice... lacking... feeding... center-specifically... ER... stress... responsive... molecules,... IRE1,... ATF6,... and... PERK,... and... measured... the... amount... of... food... and... water... consumed.... Interestingly,...the...PERK...deficient...mice...showed...a...smaller... increase...in...food...intake...than...the...others....Experiments... using...a...cell...line...derived...from...the...feeding...center...of...the... hypothalamus... also... showed... that... GADD34...affects...the... signaling...pathway...of...feeding...control...hormones....These... results...suggest...that...the...PERK...pathway...may...function,...at... least...in... part,... to... regulate... overeating...behavior...in...the... feeding...center. Polycystic...kidney...disease...is...one...of...the...most...common... inheritable...renal...disease,...characterized...by...the...formation... of... multiple... fluid-filled... renal... cysts.... This... disease... is... a... progressive...and...unfortunately...incurable...condition....The... aim...of...this...study...was...to...report...a...case...of...spontaneous... polycystic...kidney...with...progressive...chronic...renal...failure... in...an...aged...suncus....This...animal...was...investigated...the... characteristics...of...the...clinical...findings,...serum...biochemical... profiles,...inflammatory...reactions...and...histopathological... results....Serum...biochemically,...total...protein...concentrations... increased,...resulting...in...a...declined...albumin:...globulin...ratio.... Blood...urea...nitrogen...and...creatinine...concentrations...were... markedly...elevated,...indicating...the...end...stage...of...chronic... renal... failure.... Serum... amyloid... A...levels... increased... and... revealed...inflammatory...reaction...during...the...cyst...formation.... Gross...appearance...of...the...kidneys...was...bilaterally...involved... in...numerous...enlarged...parenchymal...cysts....Although...the... size...of...the...kidney...remained...unchanged,...the...surface...of...the... lesions... felt... rough... and... irregular.... Histopathologically,... multiple...cysts...were...lined...by...a...single...layer...of...epithelial... cells... or... low... cuboidal... epithelium....The... contents... were... homogenous...eosinophilic...materials...(mucopolysaccharides... or...mucoproteins)...and...these...cysts...contained...abundant... macrophages...and...vacuoles...associated...with...absorption...of... content....Although...few...macrophages...were...found...around... the...injured...glomeruli,...macrophage...infiltration...was...mainly... observed...in...the...cysts,...suggesting...some...associations...with... the...cyst...formation...and...inflammatory...reaction....There... were...also...regeneration...and...dilatation...of...renal...tubes...and... interstitial...fibrosis....The...atrophic...glomeruli...and...glomerular... capsules... were... thickened... and... hyalinized... by... dense... amorphous...substances....These...histopathological...findings... revealed... that... the... pathogenesis... of... polycystic... kidney... disease...shared...a...common...mechanistic...feature...across... species....In...the...diagnosis...of...polycystic...kidney...disease,...it...is... important...to...evaluate...renal...function...profiles...and...evidence... for...inflammation. Molecular mechanisms of overeating behavior regulated by the signaling pathway of ER stress response Ryoko Akai, Eriko Kuroda, Hisayo Hamashima, Takao Iwawaki Med....Res....Inst.,...Kanazawa...Med....Univ. Spontaneous polycystic kidneys with progressive chronic renal failure in an aged suncus (house musk shrew: Suncus murinus) Tohru Kimura, Kohei Maeie, Kengo Inaka Yamaguchi...University,...Joint...Faculty...of...Veterinary...Medicine,...Laboratory...Animal...Science P-127 P-128 --S 141-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 141 69...... The...severely...immunodeficient...NOD/Shi-scid,...IL-2RKO... (NOG)...mice...are...used...widely...to...establish...patient-derived... xenograft... (PDX)... tumor... models.... Information... on... the... lifespan...and...causes-of-death...of...NOG...mice...is...important...for... the... design... of... studies... and... the... analysis... of... results.... Therefore,...we...pathologically...analyzed...the...causes...of...death... (humanitarian...endpoint)...in...NOG...and...NOD-scid...(NOD/ ShiJic-scidJcl)...mice...in...a...lifespan...study....This...study...was... commenced...with...100...NOG...and...25...NOD-scid...mice...of...each... sex....If...severe...emaciation...or...anemia...was...observed...in...the... mice,... the... time... point... of... the... humane... endpoint... was... considered... the... lifespan... according... to... the... animal... experiment...implementation...standards...of...the...CIEA....The... survival...curves...were...drawn...using...the...Kaplan-Meier... method....NOG...mice...had...a...longer...lifespan...than...NOD-scid... mice....Male...NOG...mice...had...a...shortened...lifespan...of...50%... survival...at...67...weeks...of...age,...whereas...females...had...50%... survival...at...79...weeks....The...cause...of...death...in...NOG...mice...of... both... sexes... was... hematolymphoid... tumors,... and... the... incidence...was...high,...especially...in...males....This...study...is...the... first...study...to...analyze...the...lifespan...and...causes...of...death...in... severely...immunodeficient...mice,...and...the...lifespan...of...NOG... mice...was...unaffected...by...the...1-year...study...design. [Object] Mouse...Kidney...Parvovirus:...MKPV...(also...known... as... Murine... Chapparvovirus:... MuCPV)... is... a... virus... that... causes...various...renal...diseases...in...immunodeficient...mice....It... has...been...reported...that...the...virus...is...widely...distributed...in... animal...facilities...in...North...America...and...Australia....This... virus... has...a... genetically... different... composition... of...non- structural...protein...(NS1)...stored...in...mouse...parvovir...(MPV)... and...mouse...minute...virus...(MMV...or...Minute...virus...of...mice:... MVM)...that...infect...mice....Therefore,...it...cannot...be...detected... by...PCR...test...of...mouse...parvovirus. Since... the... epidemic... level... of... this... virus... in... Japan... is... unknown,...we...conducted...an...epidemic...survey...of...MKPV.../... MuCPV...this...time. [Method] The...specimens...were...160...mice...(61...facilities)... that...underwent...microbial...monitoring...tests...from...July...2019... to...December...2021. DNA...was...extracted...from...the...kidney...and...amplified...the... NS1...region...as...the...target....Two...pairs...of...primer...sets...were... designed...from...the...MKPV.../...MuCPV-specific...sequence... and...used...in...this...study. [Results and Discussion] As...a...result...of...PCR...screening... of...160...samples...at...61...facilities,...MKPV.../...MuCPV...was... positive...at...1...facility...and...2...samples....These...positive...mice... were...ICR...mice....Histopathological...analysis...showed...no... lesions... in... the... kidneys... in... these... animals.... Because... of... positive...cases...of...MKPV.../...MuCPV...were...found...at...one... institution,...it...was...inferred...that...the...epidemic...level...of...this... virus...in...Japan...was...relatively...low....However,...it...is...possible... that...it...may...be...potentially...present...in...Japan....We...believe... that...it...is...important...to...continue...screening...tests...for...this... virus. Analysis of lifespan and causes-of-death in NOG mice Masahiko Yasuda1 , Tomoyuki Ogura1 , Yoko Kamai1 , Misa Mochizuki1 , Yuyo Ka1 , Takeshi Endo2 , Megumi Nishiwaki2 , Riichi Takahashi1 , Kenji Kawai1 1 Central...Institute...for...Experimental...Animals,...2 CLEA...Japan,...Inc. Epidemic Survey of Mouse Kidney Parvovirus / Murine Chapparvovirus in Japan Masafumi Yamamoto1 , Ritsuki Uchida2 , Masahiko Yasuda3 , Nobuhito Hayashimoto1 1 ICLAS...Monitoring...Center,...Central...Institute...for...Experimental...Animals,...2 JAC,... 3 Pathology...Center,...Central...Institute...for...Experimental...Animals P-129 P-130 --S 142-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 142 69...... The...aim...of...this...study...is...to...generate...a...long-term...pig... rearing...isolator...and...to...confirm...the...anatomical...properties... of...the...germ-free...pig...utilizing...the...novel...isolator....The... isolator...consists...of...a...stainless-steel...cage...enclosed...in...a...soft... vinyl... chamber... and... is... sterilized... by... spraying... 0.1%... peracetic...or...chlorous...acid...solution....The...pig...used...was...a... microminipig,...one...of...the...smallest...miniature...pigs...used...for... experiments....The...phenotype...is...generated...through...natural... insemination...of...the...sow,...and...aseptic...farrowing...of...piglets... was...performed...at...110...days...gestation...via...hysterectomy.... For...the...hysterectomy...procedure,...the...uterus...was...cut... from...a...sow...under...conditions...of...inhalation...anesthesia...with... isoflurane... and... the... piglets... were... introduced... into... a... resuscitation... isolator,... a... rigid... plastic... isolator,... via... a... germicidal...trap...for...resuscitation....Piglets...were...transferred... from...the...resuscitation...isolator...to...a...nursing...isolator...that... also...consisted...of...a...hard...plastic,...and...were...reared...until...2.5... months...of...age....After...the...age...of...2.5...months,...the...piglets... were... transferred... to... a... long-term... rearing... isolator... for... further...rearing....For...sterility...evaluation,...samples...were... collected...periodically...and...cultured...in...four...medium...types... for...14...days....Two...animals...were...successfully...reared...to...the... age... of... 13... months... in... a... sterile... environment... in... these... isolators....Body...weights...of...the...germ-free...pigs...were...14.7... and...15.4,...respectively....The...germ-free...pigs...tended...to... have...larger...appendages...than...those...that...were...reared...in... the... normal... environment.... In... conclusion,... we... have... successfully...generated...a...novel...long-term...pig...rearing... flexible...film...isolator. In... many... studies... using... animals,... histopathological... evaluation... is... an... important... criterion,... and... requires... accurate,...objective,...and...quantitative...data....Conventional... histopathological...evaluation...is...performed...manually,...and... takes...a...huge...amount...of...time....Moreover,...it...results...in...poor... reproducibility...if...not...performed...by...experts....In...recent... years,...artificial...intelligence...(AI)...has...evolved...rapidly,...and... its...usefulness...for...pathological...diagnosis...has...been...widely... reported....In...particular,...deep...learning...(DL)...has...potential... to...discriminate...among...the...images...quickly...and...accurately,... resulting... in... a... shortening... of... the... time... required... for... pathological... diagnosis... with... good... reproducibility,... supporting...pathologists'...diagnosis....In...this...study,...we...have... developed...the...AI...that...automates...the...pathological...scoring... of...kidney...disease....The...observation...of...flattened...renal... tubules...is...one...of...the...histopathological...scores...for...renal... damage....Our...previous...study...showed...that...the...degree...of... flattening... itself... correlated... well... with... the... severity... of... nephropathy....This...evaluation...method...is...simpler...and...more... quantitative... than... the... conventional... histopathological... scoring,...although...it...takes...time...equal...to...or...longer....In...this... study,...we...used...a...total...of...150...sets...of...the...kidney...section... images...and...the...identical...masked...processed...images...from... mouse...models...of...renal...ischemia-reperfusion...(I/R)...injury... in...order...to...train...the...DL...models...that...perform...semantic... segmentation...of...the...proximal...tubules...with...or...without... lumina....The...renal...images...consisted...of...50...images...from... untreated...group,...50...images...from...30-min...I/R...group,...and... 50...images...from...60-min...I/R...group....Recognition...of...the... proximal...tubules...by...the...DL...models...were...sufficiently... accurate...(IoU,...0.82...with...lumina,...and...0.78...without...lumina).... By...using...the...segmented...tubules...with...and...without...lumina,... the...height...of...the...proximal...tubular...epithelial...cells...was... calculated... by... programming.... Finally,... our... AI... could... automatically...measure...the...height...of...proximal...tubular... epithelial...cells,...using...kidney...section...images...as...input....We... found...a...correlation...in...measurement...of...the...height...of...the... tubules...between...our...AI...and...manual...method....Our...AI... allows...for...a...quick,...objective...and...quantitative...evaluation... of...renal...tubular...disorders. Anatomical properties of the germ-free pig using a long-term pig rearing flexible film isolator Masayoshi Otake1 , Tsuyoshi Sato2 , Satoko Enya1 , Yusuke Ikami1 , Akihisa Kangawa1 , Kenta Kanamori3 , Makoto Amino3 , Masatoshi Shibata1 1 Shizuoka...Prefectural...Research...Institute...of...Animal...Industry,...Swine...and...Poultry...Research...Center,... 2 Nissho...Sangyo...Company,...Limited,...3 Shizuoka...Prefectural...Central...Livestock...Disease...Diagnostic...Center Automated quantification of renal tubular disorders by artificial intelligence (AI) Shuji Shimada, Kyosuke Tanimoto, Yuki Takahashi, Hayato Sasaki, Nobuya Sasaki Laboratory...of...Laboratory...Animal...Science...and...Medicine,...School...of...Veterinary...Medicine,...Kitasato...University P-131 P-132 --S 143-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 143 69...... [Background] Lactoferrin... (LF)... is... widely... used... as... a... dietary...supplement...and...a...food...additive,...and...is...known...to... have... multifaceted... actions... such... as... antibacterial/ antioxidant...action...and...immunity...activation....However,... there...are...few...studies...on...ulcer...research...of...LF...function.... This...time,...we...examined...the...possibility...of...LF's...ulcer... prevention/healing...effect...on...cysteamine-induced...duodenal... ulcer...mice...model.... [Materials and methods] C57BL/6J...adult...male...mice... were...used...throughout...the...whole...experiment....Bovine... lactoferrin...(bLF),...apolactoferrin...(aLF)...(25,...50...mg/kg,...p.o.)... and... aLF... +... bLF... mixture... (12.5,... 25... mg/kg,... p.o.)... were... treated...8...hr...before/after...treatment...of...cysteamine...(CYS)... (300...mg/kg,...po)....At...16...hours...after...drug...treatment,...the... duodenum...2...cm...on...the...pyloric...side...was...collected...and... searched...for...ulcer...score...and...gene...expression...analysis.... [Results] The...mucosal...damage...due...to...CYS...treatment... was...reduced...by...pretreatment...of...LF,...and...the...ulcer...score... was...lower...in...the...aLF...group...than...in...the...bLF...group....A... significant...decrease...was...observed...in...the...aLF...+...bLF... group....On...the...other...hand,...in...the...post-treatment...group,... the...ulcer...score...decreased...in...the...aLF...and...bLF...treatment... groups... compared... with... the... aLF... +... bLF... group.... The... expression...of...inflammatory...markers...(IL-,...IL-6)...increased... by...CYS...treatment...turned...to...decrease...by...LF...treatment.... Conversely,...changes...in...the...expression...of...mucus...factors... (Muc6,...TFF2)...and...iron...metabolism...factors...that...were... decreased...by...CYS...treatment...were...observed. [Discussion/Conclusion] LF...treatment...may...have...both... preventive... and... healing... effects... on... duodenal... ulcer,... including... anti-inflammatory... effects,... maintenance... of... mucosal...barriers,...and...normalization...of...iron...metabolism... regulation. Extracellular...vesicles...(EV)...may...not...always...reflect...the... properties...of...the...original...cells...that...secreted...them....A... prominent...example...is...the...case...of...melanoma...in...which...EV... derived...from...highly...metastatic...cells...has...a...metastasis- suppressing...effect....That...is,...the...poorly...differentiated... melanoma...Nanog+ F10...obtained...by...overexpressing...Nanog,... an...undifferentiated...state...maintaining...factor,...showed...a... higher...metastatic...potential...than...the...wild-type...strain...F10.... However,...EV...derived...from...Nanog+ F10...(Nanog+ F10-EV)... showed...a...remarkable...metastasis-suppressing...effect....The... involvement...of...macrophages...is...presumed...as...one...of...the... mechanisms....This...study...is...aimed...at...the...analysis...of...the... mechanism...of...the...macrophage...response.... Nanog+ F10-EV...was...administered...from...the...tail...vein...of... C57BL/6...mice...three...times...a...week...for...3...weeks...for...a...total... of...9...times,...and...then...the...liver...was...resected...to...analyze...the... expression...level...of...macrophage...markers....As...a...result,...the... expression...of...CD163,...one...of...the...M2-type...macrophage... markers,...decreased....The...coexistence...of...clodronate,...a... macrophage... depleting... agent,... resulted... in... the... disappearance...of...the...effect...of...Nanog+ F10-EV,...indicating... the... involvement... of... macrophages... in... the... metastasis- suppressing... effect.... Fluorescently... labeled... EV... was... prepared...and...acted...on...macrophage...cells...J774.1,...and...it... was...revealed...by...fluorescence...microscopic...observation... that...EV...was...taken...up...inside...the...cells....It...was...confirmed... that...the... expression...of...CD163...decreased...in...J774.1... in... response...to...Nanog+ F10-EV....Furthermore,...J774.1...with... different...EV...uptake...was...fractionated...by...FACS,...and...each... fraction...was...co-cultured...with...Nanog+ F10,...and...then...the... invasion... activity... of... Nanog+ F10... was... compared.... Consequently,...the...invasion...activity...was...lower...as...the... amount...of...EV...uptake...in...was...smaller.... Macrophages... are... often... categorized... into... tumor- suppressing...M1...type...and...tumor-promoting...M2...type,...and... pan-macrophages.... In... this... study,... two... markers... were... selected... per... category... and... only... CD163... showed... a... remarkable...response....In...the...future,...it...will...be...necessary... to...search...for...new...response...genes...to...Nanog+ F10-EV...in... J774.1....At...the...same...time,...it...is...essential...to...find...active... factors...in...Nanog+ F10-EV...using...CD163...and/or...new...gene... markers. Examination of preventive and healing effects of lactoferrin on duodenal ulcer in mice Masahiko Fujisawa, Takamitsu Harashima, Momoko Hirano, Kazuhisa Sugai, Yoji Hakamata Nippon...Veterinary...&...Life...Science...University,...School...of...Veterinary...Nursing...&...Technology Responses of macrophages to the metastasis-suppressing EV derived from anaplastic carcinoma cells Mikako Saito, Celine Swee May Khoo, Kyoka Asami, Takuya Henmi, Akane Morimoto Dept....Biotechnol....Life...Sci.,...Tokyo...Univ....Agricul....Technol. P-133 P-134 --S 144-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 144 69...... In... embryogenesis,...gastrulation...is... the... most... dynamic... process... for... differentiation... into... three... germ... layers.... Although...studies...using...gene...KO...mouse...models...have... elucidated... the... molecular... mechanism... underlying... the... formation...of...three...germ...layers,...the...process...is...still...not... completely...understood....To...facilitate...examination...of...three... germ...layers...formation,...we...attempted...to...establish...triple... knock-in...reporter...mouse...strain...and...ES...cell...line,...a...system... termed...Multi-color...Imaging...of...Embryonic...development... by... Reporter... Units... (MIERU)... that... allows... for... the... visualization...of...each...germ...layer...simultaneously.... Firstly,...we...created...the...homozygous...triple...knock-in...mouse... strain...named...MIERU...mouse...by...crossing...the...three...single... knock-in...mouse...strains:...Sox17-EGFP...endoderm...reporter... strain,...Otx2-tdTomato...ectoderm...reporter...strain,...and... T-TagBFP...mesoderm...reporter...stain...which...we...created... previously.... On... fluorescent... microscopic... analysis,... fluorescent...signals...were...detected...in...each...germ...layer- specific...manner...from...heterozygous...MIERU...embryos...at... embryonic...day...(E)...7.5....Because...the...litter...size...of...MIERU... mouse...was...equivalent...to...that...of...wild-type...mice,...triple... knock-in...does...not...disrupt...the...function...of...endogenous... genes. Next,...we...established...MIERU-ES...cell...lines...from...inner...cell... mass...of...homozygous...MIERU...blastocysts...under...the...2i/ LIF...condition....MIERU-ES...cells...exhibited...appropriate... development...and...germ...layer-specific...fluorescence...in...E7.5... c h i m e r i c ... e m b r y o s ... g e n e r a t e d ... b y ... t e t r a p l o i d... complementation....Further,...chimeric...embryos...successfully... produced...full-term...pup. Finally,... we... knocked... out... eight... genes... respectively... in... MIERU-ES...cells...to...generate...KO-MEIRU-ES...by...genome... editing...using...CRISPR/Cas9....Then,...we...established...the... evaluation...method...of...the...gene...functions...against...the...germ... layer...formation...using...KO-MIERU-ES...cells...combined...with... tetraploid...complementation. Collectively,...our...results...indicate...that...MIERU...system...will... be...applicable...for...studying...embryonic...development...and... regenerative... medicine... both... in vivo... and... in vitro... to... facilitate... the... research... of... laboratory... animal... science,... developmental...biology,...and...stem...cell...biology. Studies...using...germ-free...mice...have...demonstrated...that... intestinal...bacteria...play...important...functional...roles...in...host... homeostasis....However,...extrapolating...the...findings...of...these... mouse...studies...to...human...medical...research...is...hampered... by... the... evolutionary... distance... between... rodents... and... humans....Therefore,...to...advance...microbiota...research...using... non-human...primates...closely...related...to...humans,...we...have... produced...germ-free...common...marmosets...and...developed... technologies...for...their...application...in...research....To...date,...we... have...established...a...technique...for...obtaining...and...rearing... neonates...in...a...sterile...environment...and...have...succeeded...in... producing...and...maintaining...germ-free...individuals,...with... culture...tests...revealing...an...absence...of...viable...bacteria...and... fungi...for...up...to...2...years....In...addition,...we...have...developed...a... series...of...reproductive...techniques,...including...estrous...cycle... monitoring,...semen...collection,...artificial...insemination,...and... ultrasonography,...for...marmosets...in...a...sterile...isolator,...and... using... these,... have... attempted... to... breed... germ-free... marmosets....Among...individuals...aged...1.5...years...or...more,... monitoring...of...urinary...progesterone...levels...indicated...the... occurrence...estrous...cycles...in...females,...and...the...release...of... motile...sperm...was...observed...in...males....Using...pairs...of...these... sexually... mature... individuals,... we... performed... artificial... insemination.... Subsequent... monitoring... of... urinary... progesterone...levels...and...uterus...ultrasonography...indicated... the... progression... of... pregnancy... for... 6... weeks... in... one... inseminated...female....These...findings...accordingly...indicate... that...germ-free...marmosets...can...develop...to...sexual...maturity... and...reproduce...under...sterile...conditions. Medical...Research...Center,...Faculty...of...Medicine,...University...of...Tsukuba Trial of breeding germ-free common marmosets Takashi Inoue1 , Norio Okahara1 , Kenya Sato1 , Rika Kikuchi1 , Eiko Yamazaki1 , Tomoe Morioka1 , Yoko Kurotaki2 , Masami Ueno3 , Ryoko Nozu4 , Erika Sasaki1 1 Department...of...Marmoset...Biology...and...Medicine,...Central...Institute...for...Experimental...Animals...(CIEA),... 2 Center...of...Basic...Technology...in...Marmoset,...CIEA,...3 ICLAS...Monitoring...Center,...CIEA,... 4 Animal...Resource...&...Technical...Research...Center,...CIEA P-135 P-136 --S 145-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 145 69...... NOG-hIL-6...Tg...mice...are...immunodeficient...mice...expressing... the... human... interleukin... 6... gene.... These... mice... have... the... characteristic...that...differentiation...into...monocytes...and... macrophages...is...remarkably...promoted...by...the...transfer...of... hematopoietic...stem...cells.... Untreated...hIL-6...and...NOG...(7...and...26...weeks...of...age,...male... and... female,... 10... heads/group)... were... used... for... weight... measurement,...organ...weight,...and...blood...property...data.... Weight...measurements...were...taken...once...a...week....For... some...periods,...the...weight...of...hIL-6...was...lower...than...NOG.... The...average...body...weight...at...26...weeks...was...31.5...g...for... male...NOG,...30.4...g...for...hIL-6,...24.7...g...for...female...NOG,...and... 24.4...g...for...hIL-6....The...kidney...weight...of...males...of...hIL-6...was... lower...than...NOG...but...it...of...females...was...higher....For...both... males...and...females,...the...PLT...value...of...hIL-6...of...7weeks...was... lower...than...NOG,...but...it...of...26...weeks...of...age...was...higher... than...NOG. Human...IL-6...is...known...as...a...cytokine...involved...in...immune... response...and...inflammatory...response....It...has...also...been... reported...that...human...IL-6...is...involved...in...regeneration...and... thrombocytosis...when...the...liver...is...damaged,...suggesting... that...it...may...have...affected...liver...weight...and...PLT...levels....On... the...other...hand,...overproduction...of...human...IL-6...is...known... to...be...involved...in...the...development...of...many...autoimmune... and...inflammatory...diseases....In...the...future,...we...will...analyze... the...survival...rate...and...cause...of...death...on...older...mice. [Introduction] In...order...to...develop...a...treatment...for...male... infertility,...spermatogenesis...in...vitro...culturing...testis...tissue... and... germ... cells... have... been... studied... for... a... long... time.... However,... it... is... difficult... to... make... spermatogonia... differentiate...into...sperm...in...vitro....In...2011,...functional...sperm... were... produced... by... tissue... culture... of... testis... tissue... fragments...in...mouse,...however...spermatogenesis...by...tissue... culture...has...not...been...achieved...in...other...species....In...our... laboratory,...we...are...studying...on...tissue...culture...of...porcine... testis,...which...is...physiologically...close...to...humans....Here,...we... investigated... the... effects... of... using... knockout... serum... replacement...(KSR)...and...cryopreservation...of...testis...tissue... on...the...tissue...culture...of...porcine...testis. [Materials and methods] Testes...from...Yorkshire...and... Landrace...crossed...2-day-old...hybrid...piglets...were...obtained... from...pig...farm...after...castration....Shredded...testis...tissue... fragments...were...placed...on...an...agarose...gel...block,...and... culture... medium... (-MEM... +... 10%FBS... or... -MEM... +... 10%KSR)...were...added...to...1/2...to...4/5...height...of...agarose...gel... blocks.... Those... tissue... were... incubated... in... a... culture... incubator...that...was...maintained...at...34...for...about...4...weeks.... Culture...medium...were...changed...every...week...and...some... tissue...were...collected...and...analyzed...histopathologically....On... the...other...hand,...some...shredded...testis...tissue...fragments... were...immersed...in...DMEM...+...10%DMSO...+...10%KSR...+...200... mM...Trehalose...and...stored...in...liquid...nitrogen...for...about...1... month....After...that,...the...frozen...tissue...fragments...were... thawed,...and...tissue...culture...was...performed...using...the...same... procedure. [Conclusion] Stereomicroscopic... observation... of... the... cultured...tissue...revealed...that...the...bright...area...decreased... from...the...center...of...the...tissue,...which...was...consistent...with... the... necrotic... area... of... the... tissue... in... histopathological... analysis,...and...that...the...tissue...edges...were...rough...with...the... lapse... of... time.... Moreover,... tissue... cultured... in... KSR- supplemented... medium... contained... more... seminiferous... tubules...and...germ...cells...that...maintained...their...normal... structure... than... tissue... cultured... in... FBS-supplemented... medium...at...the...same...time....Therefore,...judging...from...the... culture...environment,...KSR-supplemented...medium...was... much...suitable...for...tissue...culture...than...FBS-supplemented... medium....However,...the...culture...period...was...only...about...4... weeks,... and... only... gonocytes... and... spermatogonia... were... observed.... In... addition,... no... difference... was... observed... between...the...culture...results...of...fresh...and...frozen-thawed... testis...tissue,...and...therefore...tissue...culture...of...porcine...testis... after...cryopreservation...was...applicable. Comparison of background data of NOG-hIL-6 Tg and NOG mice Yoyo Ka1 , Megumi Nishiwaki2 , Suguru Mimura2 , Takahiro Yamaguchi2 , Tomoyuki Ogura1 , Masahiko Yasuda1 , Akira Takakura1 , Riichi Takahashi1 1 Central...Institute...for...Experimental...Animals...(CIEA),...2 CLEA...Japan,...Inc Effects of culture supplements and cryopreservation of tissue on tissue culture of porcine testis Mitsuki Minaai1 , Kotono Nakamura1,2 , Mami Murakami1 , Hiroki Sakai1 , Akihiro Hirata1 , Masaki Takasu1 1 Gifu...University...Faculty...of...Applied...Biological...Sciences,... 2 Doctoral...Course...of...the...United...Graduate...School...of...Veterinary...Sciences P-137 P-138 --S 146-- The 69th Annual Meeting Japanese Association for Laboratory Animal Science 146 69...... NOG-EXL...can...be...engrafted...with...human...CD34...positive... cells...(hematopoietic...stem...cells),...as...well...as...NOG....It...is... known...that...humanized...NOG-EXL...show...more...diverse... leukocyte... differentiation... than... humanized... NOG,... with... enhanced...differentiation...of...myeloid...cells...and...CD4...T...cells.... In...this...study,...to...obtain...background...data...on...NOG-EXL... and...performed...a...comparative...analysis...with...NOG. 20... NOG-EXL... females... and... males... each,... and... 18... NOG... females...and...19...NOG...males...were...observed...once...every... day...and...body...weight...were...measured...once...a...week...from...4... to...26...weeks...of...age...(growth...curves)....Each...strain,...7...and...26... weeks...of...age,...10...males...and...10...females...were...collected... blood...and...body...weight...were...measured....After...euthanasia,... autopsy...examination...was...performed...and...organs...were... measured...(background...data)....The...results...were...analyzed... by...Student's...t-test. No...difference...was...observed...in...appearance...until...26...weeks... of...age....The...average...body...weights...of...males...and...females... were...smaller...respectively...in...NOG-EXL...at...each...week...of... age....The...growth...curves...were...similar...despite...the...weight... difference....There...were...no...differences...in...blood...properties... and...organ...weight...ratios...among...the...strains....Althought...the... body...weight...of...NOG-EXL...was...lower...body...weight...than... background... strain... NOG,... no... abnormal... findings... were... observed...until...26...weeks...of...age....We...will...collect...data...at...a... higher... age... to... confirm... whether... the... NOG-EXL... is... accompanied...by...age-related...changes...similar...to...those...of... the...background...strain...NOG,...and...also...compare...the...results... with...humanized...NOG-EXL. Sexual...maturity...does...not...occur...suddenly,...but...there...is...a... process...leading...up...to...it....This...process...usually...in...called... sexual...maturation....Therefore,...we...examined...the...sexual... maturity...of...cynomolgus...monkeys...based...on...reproduction- related...information. For...the...analysis,...45...female...and...119...male...cynomolgus... monkeys...bred...and...raised...at...the...National...Institutes...of... Biomedical...Innovation,...Health...and...Nutrition...were...used.... Timing... of... menarche,... menstrual... cycles,... and... first... pregnancy... were... examined... in... females.... In... males,... we... investigated...the...locations...of...testes,...testicular...volume...and... testosterone...levels. The...average...age...(body...weight)...at...menarche,...start...of... regular...menstruation,...and...first...pregnancy...were...3...years...3... months...(2.4...kg),...4...years...1...month...(2.6...kg),...and...5...years...5... months...(2.9...kg),...respectively....Thus,...after...the...onset...of... menarche,...it...took...about...one...year...for...the...menstrual...cycle... to...stabilize...and...one...more...year...for...pregnancy....In...males,... the...testes...had...migrated...to...the...scrotum...between...2.5...and... 4...years....In...addition,...at...about...4...years...of...age,...testicular... volume...rapidly...increased...and...then...the...blood...testosterone... concentration...increased....The...age...at...what...fertility...was... confirmed,... i.e.... the... age... at... what... male... could... a... female... pregnant,...was...about...7...years....Thus,...it...shows...that...male... sexual... maturity... begins... with... endocrine... development... around...the...age...of...4...when...the...testes...move...to...the...scrotum,... and...then...becomes...fertile...over...several...years. If...the...end...of...the...sexual...maturation...process...is...defined...as... sexual...maturity,...it...can...be...said...that...females...complete... sexually...maturation...around...5.5...years...of...age,...and...males...at... about...age...7....It...was...also...suggested...that...a...period...like... puberty... in... humans... may... also... exist... in... cynomolgus... monkeys.
